mcqs.question: What is the primary purpose of the `static` keyword in Java?

options.text: To declare instance variables
To declare variables that belong to the class itself
To improve performance by reducing memory usage
To make the code more readable

mcqs.explanation: The `static` keyword is used to denote a variable, method, or block that belongs to the class itself, rather than an instance of the class.

mcqs.question: What is the main difference between static and instance variables in Java?

options.text: Static variables are unique to each instance, while instance variables are shared among all instances
Static variables are shared among all instances, while instance variables are unique to each instance
Static variables are used for primitive types, while instance variables are used for reference types
Static variables are used for reference types, while instance variables are used for primitive types

mcqs.explanation: Static variables are shared among all instances of a class, while instance variables are unique to each instance.

mcqs.question: Can static methods access instance variables in Java?

options.text: Yes, they can access both static and instance variables
No, they can only access static variables
Yes, but only if the instance variable is declared as static
No, they can only access instance variables

mcqs.explanation: Static methods cannot directly access instance variables. They can only access static variables.

mcqs.question: When are static variables loaded into memory in Java?

options.text: When an instance of the class is created
When the class is loaded
When the program starts
When the static method is called

mcqs.explanation: Static variables are loaded into memory only once, when the class is loaded.

mcqs.question: What is the implication of using static variables in Java?

options.text: Changes to a static variable affect only the current instance
Changes to a static variable affect all instances of the class
Static variables are thread-safe
Static variables are not thread-safe

mcqs.explanation: Changes to a static variable affect all instances of the class, since static variables are shared among all instances.

mcqs.question: Can static methods be called without creating an instance of the class in Java?

options.text: Yes, they can be called without creating an instance
No, they can only be called on an instance of the class
Yes, but only if the method is declared as static
No, they can only be called on a subclass instance

mcqs.explanation: Static methods can be called without creating an instance of the class, since they belong to the class itself.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of static nested classes in Java?

options.text: To improve code organization
To reduce memory usage
To provide better encapsulation
To allow access to static members of the outer class

mcqs.explanation: Static nested classes can access static members of the outer class and can be instantiated without an instance of the outer class.
